heading: Valles Caldera National Preserve, New Mexico
label: 698v–11 Valles Caldera National Preserve, New Mexico

(b)(3)(C)(ii)(I), “” substituted for “ (commonly known as the ‘National Park Service General Authorities Act’) ()” on authority of , , , which Act enacted Title 54, National Park Service and Related Programs.section 100502 of title 54section 12(b) of Public Law 91–38316 U.S.C. 1a–7(b)Pub. L. 113–287, § 6(e)128 Stat. 3272Dec. 19, 2014

Pueblo of Santa Clara, , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–248, title I, § 104(g)114 Stat. 602July 25, 2000“(1).—In generalThe Secretary may assign to the Pueblo of Santa Clara rights to acquire for fair market value portions of the Baca ranch. The portion that may be assigned shall be determined by mutual agreement between the Pueblo and the Secretary based on optimal management considerations for the Preserve including manageable land line locations, public access, and retention of scenic and natural values. All appraisals shall be done in conformity with the Uniform Appraisal Standards for Federal Land Acquisition.“(2).—Status of land acquiredAs of the date of acquisition, the fee title lands, and any mineral estate underlying such lands, acquired under this subsection by the Pueblo of Santa Clara are deemed transferred into trust in the name of the United States for the benefit of the Pueblo of Santa Clara and such lands and mineral estate are declared to be part of the existing Santa Clara Indian Reservation.“(3).—Mineral estateAny mineral estate acquired by the United States pursuant to section 104(e) [former ] underlying fee title lands acquired by the Pueblo of Santa Clara shall not be developed without the consent of the Secretary of the Interior and the Pueblo of Santa Clara.16 U.S.C. 698v–2(e)“(4).—SavingsAny reservations, easements, and covenants contained in an assignment agreement entered into under paragraph (1) shall not be affected by the acquisition of the Baca ranch by the United States, the assumption of management by the Valles Caldera Trust, or the lands acquired by the Pueblo being taken into trust.”[Pursuant to subsec. (d)(2)(B) of this section, the provisions in section 104(g) of the Valles Caldera Preservation Act, (formerly ), set out above, remain in effect, notwithstanding the repeal of by subsec. (d)(1) of this section.]Pub. L. 106–24816 U.S.C. 698v–2(g)Pub. L. 106–248

children.heading: Eligible employee
children.label: 1 Eligible employee
children.text: The term “eligible employee” means a person who was a full-time or part-time employee of the Trust during the 180-day period immediately preceding .December 19, 2014

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 2 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Valles Caldera Fund established by section 106(h)(2) of the Valles Caldera Preservation Act.

children.heading: Preserve
children.label: 3 Preserve
children.text: The term “Preserve” means the Valles Caldera National Preserve in the State.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: State
children.label: 5 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of New Mexico.

children.heading: Trust
children.label: 6 Trust
children.text: The term “Trust” means the Valles Caldera Trust established by section 106(a) of the Valles Caldera Preservation Act.

children.heading: Designation of Valles Caldera National Preserve as a unit of the National Park System
children.label: b Designation of Valles Caldera National Preserve as a unit of the National Park System

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To protect, preserve, and restore the fish, wildlife, watershed, natural, scientific, scenic, geologic, historic, cultural, archaeological, and recreational values of the area, the Valles Caldera National Preserve is designated as a unit of the National Park System.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The boundary of the Preserve shall consist of approximately 89,900 acres of land as depicted on the map entitled “Valles Caldera National Preserve Proposed Boundary”, numbered P80/102,036C, and dated .November 4, 2014

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: B Availability of map
children.text: The map described in subparagraph (A) shall be on file and available for public inspection in appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.label: i
children.text: this section; and

children.label: I
children.text: the National Park Service Organic Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: II
children.text: the Act of ( et seq.).August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 4611

children.heading: Management coordination
children.label: B Management coordination
children.text: The Secretary may coordinate the management and operations of the Preserve with the Bandelier National Monument.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 3 fiscal years after the date on which funds are made available to implement this subparagraph, the Secretary shall prepare a management plan for the Preserve.

children.label: I
children.text: ; andsection 100502 of title 54

children.label: II
children.text: any other applicable laws.

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture;

children.label: II
children.text: State and local governments;

children.label: III
children.text: Indian tribes and pueblos, including the Pueblos of Jemez, Santa Clara, and San Ildefonso; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the public.

children.label: i
children.text: purchase from a willing seller with donated or appropriated funds; or

children.label: ii
children.text: donation.

children.heading: Prohibition of condemnation
children.label: B Prohibition of condemnation
children.text: No land or interest in land within the boundaries of the Preserve may be acquired by condemnation.

children.heading: Administration of acquired land
children.label: C Administration of acquired land
children.text: On acquisition of any land or interests in land under subparagraph (A), the acquired land or interests in land shall be administered as part of the Preserve.

children.label: i
children.text: until the date on which a management plan is completed in accordance with paragraph (3)(C), carry out the science and education program for the Preserve established by the Trust; and

children.label: I
children.text: allows for research and interpretation of the natural, historic, cultural, geologic and other scientific features of the Preserve;

children.label: II
children.text: provides for improved methods of ecological restoration and science-based adaptive management of the Preserve; and

children.label: III
children.text: promotes outdoor educational experiences in the Preserve.

children.heading: Science and education center
children.label: B Science and education center
children.text: As part of the program established under subparagraph (A)(ii), the Secretary may establish a science and education center outside the boundaries of the Preserve in Jemez Springs, New Mexico.

children.label: A
children.text: at levels and locations determined by the Secretary to be appropriate, consistent with this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent the use furthers scientific research or interpretation of the ranching history of the Preserve.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall permit hunting, fishing, and trapping on land and water within the Preserve in accordance with applicable Federal and State law.

children.heading: Administrative exceptions
children.label: B Administrative exceptions
children.text: The Secretary may designate areas in which, and establish limited periods during which, no hunting, fishing, or trapping shall be permitted under subparagraph (A) for reasons of public safety, administration, or compliance with applicable law.

children.heading: Agency agreement
children.label: C Agency agreement
children.text: Except in an emergency, regulations closing areas within the Preserve to hunting, fishing, or trapping under this paragraph shall be made in consultation with the appropriate agency of the State having responsibility for fish and wildlife administration.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: D Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this section affects any jurisdiction or responsibility of the State with respect to fish and wildlife in the Preserve.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall undertake activities to improve the health of forest, grassland, and riparian areas within the Preserve, including any activities carried out in accordance with title IV of the Omnibus Public Land Management Act of 2009 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 7301

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: B Agreements
children.text: The Secretary may enter into agreements with adjacent pueblos to coordinate activities carried out under subparagraph (A) on the Preserve and adjacent pueblo land.

children.label: A
children.text: entry, disposal, or appropriation under the public land laws;

children.label: B
children.text: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and

children.label: C
children.text: operation of the mineral leasing laws, geothermal leasing laws, and mineral materials laws.

children.label: i
children.text: no roads or buildings shall be constructed; and

children.label: ii
children.text: no motorized access shall be allowed.

children.label: i
children.text: Redondo Peak;

children.label: ii
children.text: Redondito;

children.label: iii
children.text: South Mountain;

children.label: iv
children.text: San Antonio Mountain;

children.label: xii

children.label: xiii
children.text: Indian Point;

children.label: xiv
children.text: Sierra de los Valles; and

children.label: xv
children.text: Cerros de los Posos.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: C Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply in cases in which construction or motorized access is necessary for administrative purposes (including ecological restoration activities or measures required in emergencies to protect the health and safety of persons in the area).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with Indian tribes and pueblos, shall ensure the protection of traditional cultural and religious sites in the Preserve.

children.label: i
children.text: shall provide access to the sites described in subparagraph (A) by members of Indian tribes or pueblos for traditional cultural and customary uses; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may, on request of an Indian tribe or pueblo, temporarily close to general public use 1 or more specific areas of the Preserve to protect traditional cultural and customary uses in the area by members of the Indian tribe or pueblo.

children.heading: Prohibition on motorized access
children.label: C Prohibition on motorized access
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain prohibitions on the use of motorized or mechanized travel on Preserve land located adjacent to the Santa Clara Indian Reservation, to the extent the prohibition was in effect on .December 19, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: land within the Preserve; and

children.label: ii
children.text: National Forest System land that is adjacent to the Preserve.

children.label: i
children.text: cultural and religious sites in the vicinity of the trail; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the privacy of adjacent pueblo land.

children.heading: Valid existing rights
children.label: 13 Valid existing rights
children.text: Nothing in this section affects valid existing rights.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Administrative jurisdiction over the Preserve is transferred from the Secretary of Agriculture and the Trust to the Secretary, to be administered as a unit of the National Park System, in accordance with subsection (b).

children.heading: Exclusion from Santa Fe National Forest
children.label: 2 Exclusion from Santa Fe National Forest
children.text: The boundaries of the Santa Fe National Forest are modified to exclude the Preserve.

children.heading: Memorandum of agreement
children.label: A Memorandum of agreement
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary and the Trust shall enter into a memorandum of agreement to facilitate the orderly transfer to the Secretary of the administration of the Preserve.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Existing management plans
children.label: B Existing management plans
children.text: Notwithstanding the repeal made by subsection (d)(1), until the date on which the Secretary completes a management plan for the Preserve in accordance with subsection (b)(3)(C), the Secretary may administer the Preserve in accordance with any management activities or plans adopted by the Trust under the Valles Caldera Preservation Act, to the extent the activities or plans are consistent with subsection (b)(3)(A).

children.heading: Public use
children.label: C Public use
children.text: The Preserve shall remain open to public use during the interim management period, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: A Termination
children.text: The Trust shall terminate 180 days after , unless the Secretary determines that the termination date should be extended to facilitate the transitional management of the Preserve.December 19, 2014

children.label: I
children.text: all assets of the Trust shall be transferred to the Secretary; and

children.label: II
children.text: any amounts appropriated for the Trust shall remain available to the Secretary for the administration of the Preserve.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: On termination of the Trust, the Secretary shall assume all contracts, obligations, and other liabilities of the Trust.

children.heading: Budget
children.label: aa Budget
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary and the Trust shall prepare a budget for the interim management of the Preserve.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Written concurrence required
children.label: bb Written concurrence required
children.text: The Trust shall not incur any new liabilities not authorized in the budget prepared under item (aa) without the written concurrence of the Secretary.

children.heading: Hiring
children.label: i Hiring
children.text: The Secretary and the Secretary of Agriculture may hire employees of the Trust on a noncompetitive basis for comparable positions at the Preserve or other areas or offices under the jurisdiction of the Secretary or the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Salary
children.label: ii Salary
children.text: Any employees hired from the Trust under clause (i) shall be subject to the provisions of chapter 51, and subchapter III of chapter 53, title 5, relating to classification and General Schedule pay rates.

children.heading: Interim retention of eligible employees
children.label: iii Interim retention of eligible employees
I
children.text: retained in the employment of the Trust;

children.label: II
children.text: considered to be placed on detail to the Secretary; and

children.label: III
children.text: subject to the direction of the Secretary.

children.heading: Termination for cause
children.label: iv Termination for cause
children.text: Nothing in this subparagraph precludes the termination of employment of an eligible employee for cause during the period described in clause (iii).

children.heading: Records
children.label: D Records
children.text: The Secretary shall have access to all records of the Trust pertaining to the management of the Preserve.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Effective on , the Secretary shall assume the powers of the Trust over the Fund.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Availability and use
children.label: ii Availability and use
children.text: Any amounts in the Fund as of , shall be available to the Secretary for use, without further appropriation, for the management of the Preserve.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Repeal of Valles Caldera Preservation Act
children.label: d Repeal of Valles Caldera Preservation Act

children.heading: Repeal
children.label: 1 Repeal
children.text: On the termination of the Trust, the Valles Caldera Preservation Act is repealed.

children.label: A
children.text: the authority of the Secretary of Agriculture to acquire mineral interests under section 104(e) of the Valles Caldera Preservation Act is transferred to the Secretary and any proceeding for the condemnation of, or payment of compensation for, an outstanding mineral interest pursuant to the transferred authority shall continue;

children.label: B
children.text: the provisions in section 104(g) of the Valles Caldera Preservation Act relating to the Pueblo of Santa Clara shall remain in effect; and2Text of section 104(g) is set out as a Pueblo of Santa Clara note below.2

children.label: C
children.text: the Fund shall not be terminated until all amounts in the Fund have been expended by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the Preserve;

children.label: B
children.text: the Santa Fe National Forest (other than the modification made by subsection (c)(2));

children.label: C
children.text: Bandelier National Monument; and

children.label: D
children.text: any land conveyed to the Pueblo of Santa Clara.
